title: Diosma macrosticta
taxonomy: Diosma macrosticta is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. The plant is classified as an evergreen shrub. Diosma macrosticta is closely related to the genus Zanthoxylum, but is now classified as part of the genus Diosma. The classification of Diosma macrosticta places it within the order Sapindales, which includes other flowering plants such as the soapberry and the lychee.

intro: Diosma macrosticta, also known as the Cape platthyrus or Mountain Diosma, is a shrubby, succulent plant native to the western coast of South Africa. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to promote digestion and reduce inflammation, with potential health benefits including the relief of symptoms associated with digestive issues and the improvement of overall gastrointestinal health. The bioactive constituents of Diosma macrosticta include flavonoids, such as diosmin, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to the plant's medicinal properties. Traditionally, Diosma macrosticta is prepared as a tea or a tincture, and is also used as an ingredient in some commercial digestive supplements.
